caveats: a: The OEWS occupation mix is for NAICS 327000, not 327992.
a: The estimate excludes process automation already embedded in plant controls and targets only not-yet-automated tasks.
a: The injected compensation ratio uses 2024 wages over 2022 receipts and is harmonized to the IPS basis as stated in the assignment.
rho: This is bounded implementation judgment rather than an observed adoption rate.
rho: Plants with modern historians, sensors, and ERP systems may realize more than small legacy facilities.
e: Census reports 275 employer establishments, not firms and not the target EBITDA band.
e: The injected 63-firm count is a margin-bridged ESTIMATE rather than observed EBITDA-band data.
s5: Broker marketplace data skew smaller than the lens.
s5: Economy-wide owner age does not reveal succession intentions in this industry.
s5: Strategic purchases of plants or mineral reserves may not preserve a transferable operating firm.
q: The cited pricing disclosure concerns iron ore rather than processed clays or industrial fillers.
q: Contract structure varies sharply by mineral, grade, customer concentration, and freight radius.
q: Implementation difficulty and volume changes are excluded from this primitive.
d5: Commodity mining statistics do not isolate value-added treatment performed by firms in this code.
d5: Different minerals face unrelated end-market cycles and substitution paths.
d5: Trade policy and freight economics can relocate processing without changing end demand.
o: Operator-required does not mean labor-intensive; highly automated plants still count as accountable operators.
o: Some customers could backward-integrate processing where volumes justify capital investment.

lens.customer_and_revenue_model: Repeat B2B sales of specification-controlled bulk or packaged mineral products under purchase orders, supply agreements, and customer formulas, with pricing influenced by grade, processing, energy, packaging, freight, and commodity competition.
lens.excluded_activities: Mine-site beneficiation classified in mining, captive processing plants, vertically integrated internal units without external recurring customers, shells, and non-control financing vehicles.
lens.included_activities: US lower-middle-market firms that repeatedly calcine, dead-burn, grind, blend, activate, or otherwise process clays, barite, ceramic and refractory minerals, fillers, absorbents, and other nonmetallic minerals for external industrial customers.

a.bridge: BLS measures the broader three-digit nonmetallic-mineral manufacturing sector rather than NAICS 327992, and O*NET describes one central machine-operator occupation rather than a wage-weighted industry task census. I map office, purchasing, production planning, customer service, documentation, laboratory interpretation, quality records, and portions of monitoring to AI exposure while treating driving, material handling, maintenance, sampling, setup, and physical intervention as minimally exposed. The broad sector may understate 327992's concentration in process control and overstate construction and installation work not present in this code.
caveats: The OEWS occupation mix is for NAICS 327000, not 327992.
The estimate excludes process automation already embedded in plant controls and targets only not-yet-automated tasks.
The injected compensation ratio uses 2024 wages over 2022 receipts and is harmonized to the IPS basis as stated in the assignment.
a.change_evidence: A six-digit OEWS occupation-and-wage extract for 327992, paired with task-time logs from grinding, calcining, blending, lab, dispatch, and commercial teams at 10 representative plants, would materially change the estimate.
a.rationale: The broad sector has 33.97% of employment in production, 28.00% in transportation and material moving, and 6.75% in installation, maintenance, and repair, versus 8.04% in office and administrative support and 2.74% in business and financial operations. Crushing and grinding operators monitor equipment, adjust machinery, move materials, take samples, test product, and clear jams. AI exposure is therefore meaningful in planning, records, sales, procurement, and anomaly assistance but limited across the physical core.

d5.bridge: USGS measures mined mineral production, sales, and apparent consumption for clays and barite, not constant-quality demand for the full processed output of NAICS 327992. I use 2025 clay consumption of 23.0 million tons versus 22.8 million in 2024 and increased estimated barite sales despite lower rig counts as evidence of stable-to-growing underlying physical uses, then widen for the code's diverse end markets and substitution. Mining data may overstate domestic processing when material is exported or processed elsewhere and understate demand met by imports.
caveats: Commodity mining statistics do not isolate value-added treatment performed by firms in this code.
Different minerals face unrelated end-market cycles and substitution paths.
Trade policy and freight economics can relocate processing without changing end demand.
d5.change_evidence: Inflation-adjusted shipment volumes by 327992 product class, customer end market, grade, and domestic-processing location through 2031 would materially change the range.
d5.rationale: The basket supplies construction materials, drilling fluids, paints, plastics, paper, ceramics, absorbents, and other industrial applications. Recent clay consumption and barite sales were stable to higher, while numerous substitutes and cyclical construction and drilling exposures limit confidence in growth. Diversification supports a modestly positive five-year base rather than a single-cycle forecast.

caveats: Census reports 275 employer establishments, not firms and not the target EBITDA band.
The injected 63-firm count is a margin-bridged ESTIMATE rather than observed EBITDA-band data.
e.change_evidence: A firm-level census of the 327992 population coded for external revenue share, repeat-order frequency, ownership, operating independence, and normalized EBITDA would materially improve eligibility.
e.rationale: Most independent firms in this process industry sell recurring specification-controlled inputs to external industrial customers, fitting the repeat-outsourcing lens. Ineligible cases include captive or vertically integrated units, businesses whose economics are chiefly mineral-rights ownership, and firms without transferable external customer relationships.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 327992 ($643,746,506) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 327992 ($5,807,921,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

n.detail: margin 0.1465 (margins.json[32]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Paper/Forest Products EBITDA/Sales 14.65% (mid-range for wood/paper/plastics)); revenue/employee $649,510 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 327992); class <5: 23 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,040,217 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $152,392 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 11 firms, avg revenue/firm $3,023,000 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $442,870 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 12 firms, avg revenue/firm $3,133,583 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $459,070 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 35 firms, avg revenue/firm $13,877,000 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $2,032,980 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 28 firms, avg revenue/firm $48,609,321 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $7,121,266 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 33 firms, avg revenue/firm $117,163,152 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $17,164,402 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 63

caveats: Operator-required does not mean labor-intensive; highly automated plants still count as accountable operators.
Some customers could backward-integrate processing where volumes justify capital investment.
o.change_evidence: Customer procurement studies showing the share of mineral processing brought in-house, eliminated through reformulation, or purchased as standardized material without supplier qualification would materially change the estimate.
o.rationale: Year-five demand remains demand for a physical, specification-controlled material. Customers may automate ordering and documentation, but software cannot grind, calcine, activate, blend, package, test, ship, or assume accountability for mineral chemistry and particle specifications. Only vertical integration, direct use of untreated material, or substitution removes the operator, and those effects are already reflected principally in demand.

q.bridge: Public mineral supply disclosures from larger, adjacent mineral markets document base prices, market benchmarks, freight, grade, and periodic adjustment mechanisms, not benefit sharing in 327992 LMM contracts. I infer that labor savings are not mechanically passed through when price formulas track commodity, energy, or freight inputs, but are gradually competed away during rebids and annual negotiations. This can overstate retention for undifferentiated bulk minerals and understate it for qualified specialty grades with switching costs.
caveats: The cited pricing disclosure concerns iron ore rather than processed clays or industrial fillers.
Contract structure varies sharply by mineral, grade, customer concentration, and freight radius.
Implementation difficulty and volume changes are excluded from this primitive.
q.change_evidence: Anonymized contracts and realized-price/gross-margin cohorts from independent mineral processors before and after automation, segmented by spot sales, annual purchase orders, and multi-year qualified supply, would materially move retention.
q.rationale: Industrial customers negotiate aggressively and can compare delivered cost, but product qualification, consistent particle size and chemistry, logistics, and supply reliability provide differentiation. Contract formulas typically react more directly to feedstock, market indexes, energy, and freight than to the producer's internal labor efficiency, allowing partial retention before renewal and competitive response.

caveats: This is bounded implementation judgment rather than an observed adoption rate.
Plants with modern historians, sensors, and ERP systems may realize more than small legacy facilities.
rho.change_evidence: Audited five-year deployment results from mineral processors using AI-assisted laboratory interpretation, predictive maintenance, production scheduling, order entry, and customer documentation would materially move this range.
rho.rationale: Document, order, production-planning, purchasing, maintenance-triage, customer-service, and quality-reporting workflows are implementable with bounded human approval. Realization is constrained by legacy plant systems, sparse labeled fault data, cybersecurity, customer specifications, environmental reporting, safety-critical controls, and the need to validate any AI recommendation before changing a process.

s5.bridge: BizBuySell measures reported sales of small manufacturing businesses across industries and generally below the target EBITDA band, not qualifying transfers of independent 327992 processors. Its 2,303 sold manufacturing listings during 2021-2025 establish market liquidity, while Census owner-age tables provide a broad succession signal. I translate these into a conservative five-year probability because permitting, specialized assets, reserves/feedstock contracts, customer qualification, and environmental liabilities narrow the buyer pool. Broker selection can bias observed liquidity upward; unreported strategic and family sales bias it downward.
caveats: Broker marketplace data skew smaller than the lens.
Economy-wide owner age does not reveal succession intentions in this industry.
Strategic purchases of plants or mineral reserves may not preserve a transferable operating firm.
s5.change_evidence: Closed-control-transaction counts identified to 327992 from GF Data, DealStats, PitchBook, SBA lending, and environmental permit ownership changes, matched to the independent eligible-firm denominator, would materially change the range.
s5.rationale: A functioning market for manufacturers and broad succession pressure support nontrivial availability, but only 63 target-band firms are injected and operational diligence is complex. A qualified transfer needs the plant, permits, feedstock, customer approvals, and technical team to remain intact rather than merely an asset liquidation.

principal_driver: Recurring specification-controlled B2B relationships support repeat workflows and continuing operator responsibility.
principal_weakness: Low labor intensity and a physical production/logistics workforce cap the value available from AI substitution.

run_meta.title: Ground or Treated Mineral and Earth Manufacturing

sources.supports: Reports occupation employment shares for broader NAICS 327, including production 33.97%, transportation and material moving 28.00%, office and administrative support 8.04%, and installation, maintenance and repair 6.75%.
sources.title: BLS May 2023 OEWS: Nonmetallic Mineral Product Manufacturing

sources.supports: Documents equipment observation, adjustment, maintenance, material movement, measurement, recordkeeping, inspection, sampling, testing, and jam-clearing tasks central to mineral processing.
sources.title: O*NET OnLine: Crushing, Grinding, and Polishing Machine Operators

sources.supports: Reports that physical occupations such as transportation/material moving and construction are underrepresented in Claude usage and notes that the survey is not workforce-representative.
sources.title: Anthropic Economic Index report: Cadences

sources.supports: Defines the industry as processing beyond beneficiation clays, ceramic and refractory minerals, barite, and miscellaneous nonmetallic minerals, and reports 275 employer establishments in 2023.
sources.title: Census Bureau Profile: NAICS 327992

sources.supports: Reports 2,303 sold manufacturing listings analyzed for 2021-2025 and median 2025 sale price of $650,000, establishing a proxy for manufacturing control-transfer liquidity.
sources.title: Manufacturing Business Valuation Multiples & Financial Benchmarks

sources.supports: Provides economy-wide employer-business owner characteristics including an Age of Owner table, used as broad succession context only.
sources.title: Annual Business Survey: Characteristics of Business Owners, 2022 Tables

sources.supports: Describes mineral supply agreements with base prices and adjustments tied to market prices, freight, industrial commodities, fuel, and steel producer-price indexes.
sources.title: Cleveland-Cliffs 2018 quarterly filing: customer supply agreements

sources.supports: Reports 2025 US apparent clay consumption of 23.0 million tons versus 22.8 million in 2024, increased domestic tonnage sold or used, and substitutes across filler, absorbent, and construction uses.
sources.title: USGS Mineral Commodity Summaries 2026: Clays

sources.supports: Reports that estimated barite sales increased in 2025 despite lower domestic and global rig counts, and that few large-scale substitutes exist in drilling fluids.
sources.title: USGS Mineral Commodity Summaries 2026: Barite chapter

title: Ground or Treated Mineral and Earth Manufacturing
